question: How does Apache Kafka achieve exactly-once semantics between a producer, broker, and consumer in a distributed streaming pipeline, and what specific configuration settings are required at each layer?
answer: Kafka uses a transaction coordinator and __transaction_state topic. Producers get a PID and epoch via initTransactions(); brokers reject stale epochs. The coordinator writes commit markers into data partitions. Consumers with isolation.level=read_committed only deliver messages after a commit marker. Offsets commit via sendOffsetsToTransaction(). Trade-off: each transaction adds a coordinator RTT. Without read_committed, consumers see uncommitted messages from aborted transactions.
diagram:
flowchart TD
  P[Transactional Producer] -->|FindCoordinator| TC[Transaction Coordinator Broker]
  TC -->|Writes state| TS[__transaction_state topic]
  P -->|PID, epoch, batch| B[Broker validates epoch]
  B -->|Fence zombie| Z((Stale epoch rejected))
  B -->|Data with PID| DP[Data Partition]
  TC -->|Commit marker| DP
  C[Consumer with read_committed] -->|Reads only after commit marker| DP
  P -->|sendOffsetsToTransaction| TC
  TC -->|Offset commit in same transaction| CP[Consumer Offset Partition]
  DP -->|Messages visible to app| C
  style Z fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
  style TC fill:#4dabf7,color:#fff
